channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Skill, MCP, Plugin, or just a CLI: how I pick a Claude Code extension, lightest first
item.description: I was building a plugin release with Claude Code, and the changelog draft came together nicely. Pull git log from the last tag to now, drop it under == Changelog ==. That's a procedure, so it just...

item.title: I Built an Adversarial Eval Framework and Attacked 5 LLMs — Every Single One Failed
item.description: TL;DR I built agent-eval, a framework that runs real agentic loops with tool calls against live LLM backends, then evaluates outputs through a three-tier assertion pyramid. I threw 10 adversarial...

item.title: You Don't Own the Code AI Wrote for You
item.description: AI is getting faster at generating HTML. That sounds like good news. For most people reading this, it probably is. But there is a group of users for whom faster generation is actually making...

item.title: Heart Part 7 - dalCTF 2026
item.description: Category: Web Flag: dalctf{p1mp_p1mp_h00r4y} Overview A multi-stage web challenge themed around Kendrick Lamar's m.A.A.d city / Kung Fu Kenny lore. The attack chain involved: SQL injection to...

item.title: BoxAgnts Tool System (1) — Design Motivation & Architecture Overview
item.description: The AI Agent framework landscape has reached a state one could fairly describe as oversaturated. The Python ecosystem has LangChain, CrewAI, and AutoGen; TypeScript offers the Vercel AI SDK; Go...

item.title: I built a privacy-first JSON viewer for Chrome after I got tired of waiting
item.description: A few months ago, like many devs, I noticed something off about the JSON viewer extension I'd been using for years. It had been quietly updated. The code went closed-source. Donation popups...

item.title: Why We're Changing Our Default Eval Model
item.description: We're changing the default solver model in our eval harness from Claude Sonnet 4.6 to GLM 5.1. This is the default we provide to everyone running evals on the platform. For most of the work the...

item.title: The Tokenpocalypse: What AI Token Pricing Means for SL Builders
item.description: AI token pricing is quietly rewriting the budget of every small team that touches a language model, and the cheap years are ending. TechCrunch published a piece on 7 June 2026 called Is this the...

item.title: I Rebuilt My Abandoned Dashboard by Starting With Docs — Kumachi Back Office
item.description: This is a submission for the GitHub Finish-Up-A-Thon Challenge. What I Built Kumachi Back Office is the private operating system for my creative and professional world. It is a founder command...

item.title: Before You Write a Single Line: How to Plan Your API Like a Senior Engineer
item.description: In my second week at work, I watched a senior engineer spend 3 days refactoring an API that took 1 day to build. The endpoint worked. The logic was correct. The code was clean. But halfway through...

item.title: NASA will wear high-tech Prada long johns to the Moon
item.description: We've seen Axiom Space and Prada's collaboration on the Axiom Extravehicular Mobility Unit (AxEMU) spacesuit. Now the company has revealed the Liquid Cooling and Ventilation Garment (LCVG) that...

item.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
item.description: We're likely to see more price increases as the big AI companies plan to go public.

item.title: Amazing Digital Dentures (a failed project)
The 7 biggest storylines from Summer Game Fest 2026
item.description: The 2026 edition of Summer Game Fest just wrapped up, and it was surprisingly hectic. The nearly week-long event came at a challenging time for the games industry, and for the most part the big...

item.title: Persona 6 exists, and that’s all we know
item.description: This year's Summer Game Fest turned out to be a good one for fans of Japanese RPGs. First, the conclusion of the Final Fantasy VII remake trilogy was announced at SGF Live, and now we have the...

item.title: Notion restores access to Anthropic after service disruption
item.description: Notion's head of product said he was "astonished" at “the amount of people RT-ing this."

item.title: Microsoft’s Xbox 25th anniversary console comes in translucent green
item.description: Microsoft has created a special edition Xbox Series X to celebrate 25 years of the console. The Xbox 25th-anniversary console takes design cues from the original Xbox console, with both the...

item.title: Minecraft Dungeons 2 gets a September release date
item.description: Minecraft Dungeons 2, a sequel to Microsoft's dungeon crawler spinoff Minecraft Dungeons, will be released on September 29th. The company originally revealed the game in a brief trailer in March,...

item.title: Fable launches in late February after recent delay
item.description: Just a few days after pushing Fable out of 2026, Microsoft showed off more footage of Fable, the first new entry in the storied RPG franchise since 2010's Fable III, at its Xbox Games Showcase on...

item.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved arrives July 28th
item.description: As part of its Xbox Games Showcase on Sunday, Microsoft revealed new details about Halo: Campaign Evolved, the upcoming remake of Halo: Combat Evolved's campaign mode. The remake will debut on...

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day isn’t coming to the PS5
item.description: Apparently, the "return of Xbox" means a retreat from other platforms. At its Xbox Games Showcase today, Microsoft revealed that Gears of War: E-Day - which was previously rumored for a PS5 launch...

item.title: The Verge Weekend Questionnaire
item.description: Have you ever wondered what the most indispensable app is for your favorite musician or how the world’s tech CEOs stay focused? Well, that’s the sort of thing we aim to uncover in our Verge...

item.title: Xbox Games Showcase 2026: All the news and trailers
item.description: The console industry is in a weird place, and both Xbox and PlayStation have a chance to change the narrative a bit with their showcases at Summer Game Fest. Sony did that by focusing on the...

item.title: OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
item.description: "Chat is dead" — at least, according to a senior OpenAI employee.

item.title: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
item.description: Even with Lockdown Mode, ChatGPT could be still vulnerable to prompt injections, but the goal is to reduce the likelihood that sensitive data gets shared in the process.

item.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
item.description: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

item.title: Sriram Krishnan is leaving his role as White House AI advisor
item.description: Krishnan is reportedly starting a new institution to continue shaping Trump's AI policy.

item.title: The Trump administration might take an equity stake in OpenAI
item.description: President Donald Trump said he's discussing deals "where the American people can benefit from the success of AI."

item.title: New ChatGPT Lockdown Mode Limits Tools That Could Enable Data Exfiltration
item.description: OpenAI has begun rolling out a new Lockdown Mode to ChatGPT for eligible personal accounts to reduce the risk of data exfiltration arising from prompt injection attacks. The feature is primarily...

item.title: Crypto-Funded Chinese Peptide Labs Are Booming
item.description: Plus: Hackers use Meta’s AI bots to hack Instagram accounts, Anthropic helps NSA hackers, a decades-long GPS satellite mystery may have been solved, and more.
